debug_info.goal: ⊢ ∃ s, s.Infinite ∧ s.Pairwise fun m n => (2 ^ ↑n - 3).Coprime (2 ^ ↑m - 3)

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)

have h_main : ∃ (s : Set ℕ+), s.Infinite ∧ s.Pairwise fun m n ↦ Nat.Coprime (2 ^ n.val - 3) (2 ^ m.val - 3) := by
    use Set.range fun n : ℕ+ => (⟨2 * n.val + 2, by
      have h : 2 * n.val + 2 > 0 := by
        have h₁ : n.val > 0 := by exact n.property
        linarith
      exact h
      ⟩ : ℕ+)

      apply Set.infinite_range_of_injective
      intro n m h
      simp only [Set.mem_range, Prod.mk.injEq] at h
      simp_all [PNat.mk.injEq]
      <;> omega
    · -- Prove that every pair of elements in the set is coprime
      intro x y hxy
      rcases x with ⟨x, hx⟩
      rcases y with ⟨y, hy⟩
      simp only [Set.mem_range, exists_prop] at hxy
      rcases hxy with ⟨n, rfl⟩
      rcases hxy with ⟨m, rfl⟩
      have h₁ : Nat.Coprime (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) := by
        have h₂ : 2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) % 5 = 4 := by
          have h₃ : ∀ k : ℕ, 2 ^ (2 * k + 2) % 5 = 4 := by
            intro k
            induction k with
            | zero => norm_num
            | succ k ih =>
              simp [pow_add, pow_mul, Nat.mul_mod, Nat.pow_mod, ih]
              <;> norm_num <;> omega
          exact h₃ m.val
        have h₃ : 2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) % 5 = 4 := by
          have h₄ : ∀ k : ℕ, 2 ^ (2 * k + 2) % 5 = 4 := by
            intro k
            induction k with
            | zero => norm_num
            | succ k ih =>
              simp [pow_add, pow_mul, Nat.mul_mod, Nat.pow_mod, ih]
              <;> norm_num <;> omega
          exact h₄ n.val
        have h₄ : (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) % 5 = 1 := by
          have h₅ : 2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) % 5 = 4 := h₂
          have h₆ : 2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) ≥ 3 := by
            have h₇ : 2 * m.val + 2 ≥ 2 := by
              have h₈ : m.val ≥ 1 := by exact m.property
              linarith
            have h₉ : 2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) ≥ 2 ^ 2 := by
              exact pow_le_pow_right (by norm_num) (by linarith)
            linarith
          omega
        have h₅ : (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) % 5 = 1 := by
          have h₆ : 2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) % 5 = 4 := h₃
          have h₇ : 2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) ≥ 3 := by
            have h₈ : 2 * n.val + 2 ≥ 2 := by
              have h₉ : n.val ≥ 1 := by exact n.property
              linarith
            have h₉ : 2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) ≥ 2 ^ 2 := by
              exact pow_le_pow_right (by norm_num) (by linarith)
            linarith
          omega
        have h₆ : Nat.gcd (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) = 1 := by
          have h₇ : (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) % 5 = 1 := h₄
          have h₈ : (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) % 5 = 1 := h₅
          have h₉ : ∀ d : ℕ, d ∣ (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) → d ∣ (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) → d ∣ 1 := by

            have h₁₂ : d ∣ (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) := h₁₀
            have h₁₃ : d ∣ (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) := h₁₁
            have h₁₄ : d ∣ 1 := by

              have h₁₅ : d ∣ (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) := h₁₂
              have h₁₆ : d ∣ (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) := h₁₃

                have h₂₈ : (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) % 5 = 1 := h₅
                -- Use the fact that the numbers are congruent modulo 5 to deduce that d cannot divide both

                have h₃₀ : d ∣ (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) := h₁₃
                -- Use the fact that the numbers are congruent modulo 5 to deduce that d cannot divide both

                have h₃₂ : (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) % d = 0 := by
                  exact Nat.mod_eq_zero_of_dvd h₃₀
                -- Use the fact that the numbers are congruent modulo 5 to deduce that d cannot divide both
                have h₃₃ : d ∣ (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) := h₁₂
                have h₃₄ : d ∣ (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) := h₁₃
                -- Use the fact that the numbers are congruent modulo 5 to deduce that d cannot divide both
                have h₃₅ : (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) % 5 = 1 := h₄
                have h₃₆ : (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) % 5 = 1 := h₅
                -- Use the fact that the numbers are congruent modulo 5 to deduce that d cannot divide both
                omega

            exact h₁₄

            have h₂₄ : Nat.gcd (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) ∣ (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) := h₂₀
            have h₂₅ : Nat.gcd (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) ∣ (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) := h₂₁
            have h₂₆ : Nat.gcd (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3) ∣ 1 := h₉ (Nat.gcd (2 ^ (2 * m.val + 2) - 3) (2 ^ (2 * n.val + 2) - 3)) h₂₄ h₂₅
            exact h₂₆
